llm_verdict.message:
The kernel.kptr_restrict setting has been fully remediated:

* Existing `kernel.kptr_restrict` lines in `/etc/sysctl.d/*.conf` were backed up and commented out, preventing duplicate or conflicting settings.
* A new persistent configuration file `/etc/sysctl.d/99-kernel-kptr-restrict.conf` was created with the line:
  ```
  kernel.kptr_restrict = 2
  ```
* The runtime value was immediately applied using `sysctl -w kernel.kptr_restrict=2`.
* All sysctl configurations were reloaded with `sysctl --system` to ensure the new setting is active.

The system now enforces the recommended kernel pointer restriction (value 2) both at runtime and after reboot, satisfying the security rule without affecting SSH or other critical services.
